description: Water may be 'shiloa' when no other substance is mixed into it for the purpose at hand. A tone or intention can be pure in the same sense: one quality is present without another running through it. The word describes composition, never a person's moral worth.
articulatory_notes: Raise the tongue blade toward the postalveolar region for voiceless 'sh' and pass air through the narrow channel into high-front unrounded 'i'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and release along its sides into stressed rounded true-mid back 'o'. Keep final central 'a' in a separate syllable by opening the jaw and releasing the lip rounding after the hiatus.
sound_symbolism: The narrow airflow of 'shi' opens through rounded 'lo' into a separate final 'a'. That gradual widening can suggest a mixture being clarified until one contour remains audible.
usage_notes: When exact composition matters, the relevant substance, method, or source standard belongs beside the Phi description. 'hiso' (clean) says unwanted material has been removed; 'shiloa' says the named mixture is absent in the respect being discussed.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: an unmixed colour, tone, or formal quality
semantic_domains.cognition: one intention, factor, or line of thought considered without another mixed through it
semantic_domains.physical: material treated as unmixed under the stated conditions
